content: EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-001 (AA95 Out-of-Gas) — REJECTED: Bundler self-grief only. Attacker can only abort their own bundle, not steal from third parties. No victim fund loss possible.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-002 (Reentrancy via innerHandleOp Exec.call) — REJECTED: Deposit inflation requires pre-existing ETH. Net position unchanged since attacker only inflates their own deposit. Self-harm only, no victim fund loss.
EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-003 (Reentrancy via Paymaster postOp) — REJECTED: Self-harm only. postOp revert sets mode==postOpReverted, bypassing prefund check. Cannot manipulate victim deposits.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-004 (Prefund-Too-Low via Paymaster) — REJECTED: Causally impossible. postOp revert sets mode==postOpReverted, which falls through to actualGasCost=prefund and bypasses the INNER_REVERT_LOW_PREFUND check at line 731.
EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-005 (Beneficiary Revert) — REJECTED: Only bundler controls beneficiary parameter. Cannot be exploited by third party. Self-grief only.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-006 (Unchecked Gas Cost Overflow) — REJECTED: Mathematically possible but physically impossible on EVM. actualGas * gasPrice produces ~10^32, far below 2^256. No realistic overflow.
EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-007 (Missing nonReentrant on incrementNonce) — REJECTED: Nonce manipulation causes DoS/griefing only. Cannot lead to fund theft since nonce doesn't control deposit amounts.
EntryPoint 0.7.0 VULN-008 (_compensate Reentrancy) — REJECTED: Beneficiary is bundler-controlled. Only bundler can set themselves as beneficiary. Cannot be exploited by third party for fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 confirmed SAFE for user-to-user fund theft. All external calls use msg.sender-keyed deposit accounting. No path found for cross-account deposit manipulation or theft. Cross-validated by reentrancy analysis, deposit flow analysis, and prefund mechanism review.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 storage collision search — REJECTED: No exploitable collision. C3 linearization properly isolates slots (ReentrancyGuard=0, StakeManager=1, NonceManager=2). No slot shadowing in assembly. No library storage corruption.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 delegatecall search — REJECTED: Exec.call uses regular call (not delegatecall) — user contract runs in own storage context. delegateAndRevert reverts state changes atomically. No fund theft via delegatecall possible.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 CREATE2 collision search — REJECTED: EntryPoint does not use CREATE2 directly; factory controls deployment. AA10/AA13/AA14/AA15 checks robust. Factory with predictable salt causes DoS only, not fund theft. No direct CREATE2 collision theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 replay attack search — REJECTED: Cross-chain replay protected (chainid in getUserOpHash). Double execution protected (nonce increment). Paymaster signatures unique per userOpHash. No valid replay exploit for fund theft found.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 access control search — PARTIAL: incrementNonce lacks access control but only causes DoS/griefing. delegateAndRevert enables state reading but changes revert. Account deposit theft via paymaster NOT POSSIBLE (architecturally separated). No access control bypass for fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 signature malleability search — REJECTED: EntryPoint never validates signatures directly (no ecrecover). userOpHash includes all fields including paymasterAndData, so signature changes change hash. Nonce prevents replay. No signature malleability exploit for fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 gas griefing search — REJECTED: callGasLimit properly bounds nested calls. preOpGas penalty mechanism protects bundler. actualGasCost capped by prefund via postOp revert path (mode==postOpReverted). No gas griefing leading to victim fund loss.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 block/timing manipulation search — REJECTED: validUntil/validAfter controlled by account signature (attacker cannot forge). unstakeDelaySec enforced on-chain. block.timestamp cannot bypass storage reads. No block/timing exploit for fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 cross-function race condition search — REJECTED: Validation-then-execution atomic split. WithdrawTo uses msg.sender-keyed deposit. postOp revert reverts entire bundle. No race condition enables victim fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 ERC-165 interface confusion search — REJECTED: XOR composite interfaceId computationally infeasible to collide. Call targets fixed (sender/paymaster). Context only flows back to originating paymaster. No interface confusion exploit for fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 self-destruct/deployment attack search — REJECTED: Atomic validation-execution split. AA10 prevents sender address reuse. Deposit decremented before execution. Self-destruct cannot bypass validation or steal deposits. No deployment attack for fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 paymaster deposit theft deep dive — REJECTED: During postOp, msg.sender=EntryPoint. withdrawTo uses deposits[msg.sender]=deposits[EntryPoint]. Paymaster cannot manipulate msg.sender. Context bytes are opaque. Account deposit only deducted when paymaster==address(0). No paymaster-to-victim deposit theft possible.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 aggregator manipulation search — REJECTED: validateSignatures is view function (cannot modify state). Two-phase validation (aggregator + account). AA24 expectedAggregator check cannot be bypassed. Malicious aggregator can only cause DoS, not fund theft.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 mempool front-running search — REJECTED: All UserOps require valid signature from sender private key. Nonce mechanism prevents replay. initCode AA14/AA15 checks prevent factory front-running. No MEV vector leads to fund theft from victim.

EntryPoint 0.7.0 comprehensive exploit search — FINAL RESULT: NO FUND THEFT FROM VICTIMS. All 14 vulnerability classes searched and rejected: force-send ETH (protected by receive()), memory corruption (EVM reverts), bytecode OOG (DoS only), revert data handling (safe due to 32-byte return check), unchecked returns (all checked), integer underflow (all protected), locking attacks (DoS only), hash collision (standard keccak), simulation divergence (not implemented), balance manipulation (DoS only), zero-address (harmless lockup), ERC-20 (by design), reentrancy (nonReentrant protected), calldata parsing (safe).

Enzyme Onyx ENZYME-001 (Reentrancy in Shares ERC20 hooks) — DIALECTIC REJECTED: Attacker must deploy malicious token and call Shares.transfer() themselves. Self-harm only, no third-party theft. Revises to Informational.

Enzyme Onyx ENZYME-002 (Global ownership transfer to address(0)) — CONFIRMED: Ownable2Step allows ownership=address(0) which permanently bricks factory upgrades. No fund theft, but protocol insolvency. Revises to Medium.

Enzyme Onyx ENZYME-003 (ERC7540LikeRedeemQueue admin freeze funds) — DIALECTIC REJECTED: Admin is the attacker. User can cancel after minRequestDuration. Admin trust issue, not third-party code exploit. Revises to Medium.

Enzyme Onyx ENZYME-004/005/006/009 (out-of-order execution, share price manipulation, batch revert, gas griefing) — ALL DIALECTIC REJECTED: Admin trust issues or griefing only. No third-party fund theft. All revise to Low.
Enzyme Onyx ENZYME-007 (MgmtFeeTracker not initialized) — DIALECTIC REJECTED: Deployment error only. No third-party attacker. Revises to Low.
Enzyme Onyx ENZYME-008 (AuthTransfer bypass) — DIALECTIC REJECTED: Handler compromise required (external key issue). Not a code vulnerability. Revises to Informational.

Enzyme Onyx confirmed SAFE for third-party user-to-user fund theft. All 9 findings require admin/owner/handler key compromise. Protocol designed for trust-model with admin secured via multisig/DAO. No code exploit enables third-party attacker to steal from unwilling victim.

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED: ERC7540LikeDepositQueue controller bypass + shares minting inflation — REJECTED: No requestDepositFor() function exists in contract. executeDepositRequests is onlyAdminOrOwner. The described front-running mechanism is impossible. Findings revised to Informational.

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED: Beacon proxy upgrade no implementation validation — REJECTED: setImplementation is onlyOwner. No path exists without owner key compromise. address(0) impl would self-revert. Admin-trust issue, not a code exploit. Finding revised to Informational.

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED: Fee tracker precision HWM manipulation via SECONDS_IN_YEAR rounding — REJECTED: SECONDS_IN_YEAR = 31557600 (365.25 days) already accounts for leap years. Even if not, only fund manager sets rates — self-harm only. No third-party manipulation path. Finding revised to Informational.

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED: Shares mint/burn authorization burnFor decoupled from asset transfer — REJECTED: burnFor is onlyRedeemHandler. Attacker needs approval/operator status granted by victim or admin first. Self-harm/trust assumption, not third-party exploit. Finding revised to Low (self-harm).

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED: ValuationHandler untrackedPositionsValue + rate expiry manipulation — REJECTED: All rate and value updates require onlyAdminOrOwner. External position trackers query on-chain value. No third-party manipulation path without admin compromise. Finding revised to Informational.

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED: CCIP cross-chain message spoofing/fee manipulation — REJECTED: onlyWalletsManager guard restricts cross-chain calls. If attacker is walletsManager they are admin-trust. If attacker is not walletsManager, they cannot spoof messages. Admin-trust finding. No third-party exploit.

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED: LinearCreditDebtTracker timestamp/start manipulation — REJECTED: All time-dependent operations (accrual, write-down) require onlyAdminOrOwner. No third-party can manipulate timestamps or start times. Finding revised to Informational.

Enzyme Onyx EXPANDED SEARCH FINAL RESULT: 8 vulnerability classes searched across 13 sub-classes. ALL findings require admin/owner/handler key compromise or are non-existent mechanisms. No third-party exploitable code vulnerability found that results in user fund theft. Protocol confirmed SAFE for external attackers — security boundary is admin trust model.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: EIP-4844 blob transactions — REJECTED. BLOBHASH opcode unused in EntryPoint; blob data transparent to execution, not used in validation. No third-party path to fund theft.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: EIP-1153 transient storage — REJECTED. EntryPoint uses no TLOAD/TSTORE; validation/postOp isolated; transient cleared between calls. No third-party path to fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: ERC-7702 account proxy — REJECTED. Requires existing nonce>=1 to set code; sender.code.length check prevents redeployment (AA10); SenderCreator uses CREATE not affected. No third-party path.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: Memory alignment attacks — REJECTED. EntryPoint uses aligned memory access; no BYTE opcode tricks; assembly properly manages free pointer. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Gas price manipulation via block builder — REJECTED. UserOp gas price derived from user's maxFeePerGas/maxPriorityFeePerGas; bundler collusion only causes DoS, not theft. No third-party path.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: EOF jump table manipulation — REJECTED. EntryPoint compiled as legacy (Solidity 0.8.23); no EOF opcodes; standard CALL works identically. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Calldata boundaries / method signature routing — REJECTED. userOpHash binds validation to specific callData; methodSig extraction has length check; can't modify UserOp after signing. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Signature scheme divergence (EIP-1271, EIP-2098) — REJECTED. EntryPoint only reads packed validationData; signature processing entirely in account's validateUserOp; third party cannot forge. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Gas stipend reliance — REJECTED. All calls use explicit gas forwarding (verificationGasLimit, callGasLimit, paymasterPostOpGasLimit); _compensate forwards all gas. No third-party path.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: SSTORE clearing/refund exploitation — REJECTED. Deposit updates are arithmetic (add/sub), not clearing to zero; prefund tracking uses actualGas*gasPrice. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Paymaster deposit credit timing — REJECTED. Deposit debited before validatePaymasterUserOp (line 535); postOp revert paths correctly charge paymaster; no double-refund window. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Gas calculation edge cases (CREATE, EXT*, SLOAD, SSTORE) — REJECTED. actualGas measured identically in simulation and execution; _validatePrepayment code paths same; gasleft() accurate in both. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Baseline gas calculation edge case — REJECTED. actualGas = preGas - gasleft() + opInfo.preOpGas; identical in simulation vs execution. Bundler controls callGasLimit, not third party. No fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: postOp revert + actualGas tracking — REJECTED. When postOp reverts with postOpReverted mode, actualGasCost capped at prefund (line 733-735). Bundler receives LESS than expected, not more. No fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Gas price update race — REJECTED. Signature is over userOp.hash() which includes maxFeePerGas. Third party cannot produce valid signature for modified gas price without sender private key. No fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Prefund calculation precision loss / overflow — REJECTED. All gas values validated <= type(uint120).max (line 639); overflow in unchecked reverts tx, not theft; underflow caught by AA21 deposit check at line 501-504. No fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: Beneficiary revert in receive() — REJECTED. Beneficiary is per-batch (bundler's choice). If it reverts, entire handleOps reverts atomically — all deposit deductions revert. No fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: HandleOps batch OOG — REJECTED. INNER_OUT_OF_GAS causes entire bundle revert via FailedOp; all deposit deductions revert atomically. Remaining ops' deposits restored. No fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: SimulateHandleOp gas divergence — REJECTED. EVM impl differences cause simulation to underestimate gas -> actual OOG -> bundle revert. Bundler loses gas, no third party theft. No fund theft.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: initCode COINBASE/TIMESTAMP divergence — REJECTED. If factory deploy code reads block.coinbase/timestamp and they differ, sender address mismatch causes revert at AA14. No ETH leaves deposits. No fund theft.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: SenderCreator deployment race — REJECTED. Factory determines sender address; AA14 strict validation (sender1 != sender reverts). Cannot front-run. No third-party path.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: NonceManager slot/key bypass — REJECTED. nonceSequenceNumber[sender][key] is per-sender-per-key isolated; attacker can only consume their own nonce sequence. No third-party path.

EntryPoint CREATIVE: handleOps retry with different gas — REJECTED. Signature covers gas parameters via userOpHash; modifying gas params after signing invalidates signature (AA23). No third-party path.
EntryPoint CREATIVE: Cross-chain signature replay — REJECTED. userOpHash includes block.chainid (line 367). Signature from chain A cannot be replayed on chain B. No third-party path.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Sandwich attack on share price — REJECTED. Share price fetched ONCE at batch start (ERC7540LikeRedeemQueue:266). Third party cannot trigger batch execution or manipulate price. No fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Uint256 underflow when totalSupply=0 — REJECTED. __updateShareValue skips division when sharesSupply==0 (sets netShareValue_=0). getSharePrice returns 10^18 when value=0, not exploitable. No fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Mint shares to address(0) — REJECTED. mintFor accepts any _to, no zero-address check. But only DepositHandler (admin-controlled) can call. Third party cannot trigger mint. Admin-trust.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Reentrancy into ERC7540 queue during execute — REJECTED. executeDepositRequests loop processes all requests, then ONE safeTransfer after loop. No callback during iteration. No reentrancy path. No fund theft.
Enzyme CREATIVE: Vault shutdown/pause function — REJECTED. No pause/unpause function exists in Shares.sol or queues. Function not present. No third-party path.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Fee claim dilution before batch — REJECTED. claimFees is onlyAdminOrOwner. Third party cannot claim fees before batch to inflate share price. Admin-trust issue, not third-party exploit.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Position tracker removal gap — REJECTED. removePositionTracker removes from set; getPositionValue no longer queries it. Share price NOT auto-updated. Requires admin to call updateShareValue() manually. Admin-trust.

Enzyme CREATIVE: ERC20 approve race — REJECTED. Queue uses authTransferFrom requiring isRedeemHandler, not standard approve. Deposit uses safeTransferFrom for assets, not shares. Standard approve race not applicable. No fund theft.
Enzyme CREATIVE: 1 wei vault redemption — REJECTED. executeRedeemRequests line 195: require(userAssets > 0) reverts if converts to 0. Prevents rounding exploit. No fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: LP token manipulation via DEX — REJECTED. AccountERC20Tracker returns ERC20 balance * rate. Rate set by admin via setAssetRate(). No Chainlink oracle. Third party cannot manipulate rate. Admin-trust.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Stale rate revert in convertAssetAmountToValue — REJECTED. Rate expiry causes revert but admin sets rate via setAssetRate(). Third party cannot cause rate expiry. DoS only, not fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: SyncDepositHandler stale price bypass — REJECTED. maxSharePriceStaleness set by admin via setMaxSharePriceStaleness(). Third party cannot manipulate. Admin-trust issue, not third-party exploit.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Fee-on-transfer token support gap — REJECTED. Comment at SyncDepositHandler:29 explicitly states may not support fee-on-transfer. Asset deposited by user; third party would need to control asset. Not third-party exploit.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Precision loss in entrance/exit fee calculation — REJECTED. feeSharesAmount = (grossSharesAmount * feeBps) / 10000; if rounds to 0, no fee collected. Benefits tiny depositors, not exploitable attack. Rounding edge case, not fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: ContinuousFlatRatePerformanceFeeTracker HWM not reset on loss — REJECTED. If share price drops below HWM, no performance fee and HWM NOT reset. Fee waived until price recovers. Attackers want price to drop to avoid fee, but cannot trigger settlement. No fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: LinearCreditDebtTracker duration=0 overflow — REJECTED. Code comment says duration=0 means discrete value change at start but calculation reverts at line 202. Duration set by admin. Causes revert (DoS), not fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: ContinuousFlatRateManagementFeeTracker timestamp manipulation — REJECTED. Uses block.timestamp which miners/validators can manipulate within +/-15 seconds. Affects fee amount but fee goes to admin/recipient. Miner MEV, not significant fund theft.

Enzyme CREATIVE: Opaque calldata context to paymaster — REJECTED. Paymaster returns arbitrary bytes as context, bound to userOpHash at validation time (line 627). Paymaster is staked+trusted, EntryPoint never interprets context. No third-party path.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: EntryPoint->Enzyme CCIP via paymaster/account — REJECTED. onlyWalletsManager guard blocks unauthorized calls; paymaster postOp has no user funds at stake. No third-party path.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: Bundler order manipulation of redemption queue — REJECTED. executeRedeemRequests requires onlyAdminOrOwner; bundler cannot touch queue. No third-party path.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: Flashbots + Enzyme MEV share price sandwich — REJECTED. updateShareValue() is onlyAdminOrOwner; no MEV path to manipulate pricing. No third-party path.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: Account code as Enzyme handler bypass — REJECTED. Handler registration needs admin; authTransfer only moves handler's own shares. No third-party path.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: Cross-contract reentrancy EntryPoint <-> Enzyme — REJECTED. EntryPoint has nonReentrant; self-harm only, no third-party path. No cross-protocol reentrancy exploit.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: Unchecked return data on cross-contract calls — REJECTED. All calls use checked patterns (Exec.call, SafeERC20, functionCallWithValue). No unchecked return data exploit.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: CREATE2 salt collision between EntryPoint factory and Enzyme factory — REJECTED. Different bytecode prevents equivalence; birthday attack infeasible. No third-party path.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: EIP-1271 + SharesTransferValidator bypass — REJECTED. Independent validation systems with no interaction point. No third-party path.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: CCIP message processing silent failure — REJECTED. Try-catch intentional design; tokens remain in user wallet. Informational finding, not fund theft.
CROSS-PROTOCOL: DepositorWallet gas griefing — REJECTED. Requires admin allowlist of malicious target first. Griefing only, not fund theft.

CREATIVE SEARCH ROUND 2 FINAL RESULT: 50+ novel vectors searched across EntryPoint (24 new), Enzyme Onyx (20 new), and Cross-Protocol (10 new). ALL findings require admin/owner/handler key compromise, result in DoS/revert only, or describe non-existent mechanisms. NO third-party exploitable code vulnerability found that results in user fund theft. Both protocols CONFIRMED SAFE for external attackers.
